title: Systema Perturbation-Response Evaluation Resources

data_urls.access_notes: Use upstream archive instructions for exact reproduction.

instructions: Inspect the Systema repository for evaluation definitions.
Download the source-code archive or data resources through upstream instructions.
Preserve reference choices and systematic-variation controls in SPECTRA manifests.

recommended_axes.name: systematic_variation_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity over perturbation-independent systematic variation signatures.

recommended_axes.name: gene_network_distance
recommended_axes.definition: Pathway, GO, or interaction-network distance between perturbation targets.

recommended_axes.name: batch_dataset_context
recommended_axes.definition: Shared dataset, assay, batch, or cell-line context used as a control axis.

useful_for: Auditing whether perturbation models learn perturbation-specific signal or systematic variation.
Testing hidden biological and technical confounders.
Mechanism-debt follow-ups after surface perturbation support curves.
leakage_risks: Reference-choice leakage can make models look better than they are.
Batch and systematic variation can dominate perturbation-specific signal.
Gene-expression-derived axes may be post-hoc if computed on held-out responses.
